presentation.chiefComplaint: Lower belly pain and a bit of spotting

history.hpi: A woman of reproductive age with one day of crampy left lower abdominal pain and light vaginal spotting. She assumes it is a bad period, though her last period was about seven weeks ago and lighter than usual. She felt briefly lightheaded standing up and has a vague ache in her right shoulder. She is not on reliable contraception.
redFlags: Possible ectopic pregnancy: childbearing age with abdominal pain and a late/abnormal period
Referred shoulder-tip pain suggesting intraperitoneal blood (diaphragmatic irritation)
Orthostatic lightheadedness — early sign of intra-abdominal bleeding
Mandatory pregnancy test before any other workup

redFlagConcepts.flag: Possible ectopic pregnancy: childbearing age with abdominal pain and a late/abnormal period
Referred shoulder-tip pain suggesting intraperitoneal blood (diaphragmatic irritation)
Orthostatic lightheadedness — early sign of intra-abdominal bleeding
Mandatory pregnancy test before any other workup

expert.esiRationale: The trap: a young woman with mild abdominal pain and spotting and a soft blood pressure is easily under-triaged to ESI 3 as 'period pain.' But a late/abnormal period in a sexually active woman with referred shoulder-tip pain and orthostatic lightheadedness is a possible ruptured ectopic pregnancy — immediately life-threatening intra-abdominal bleeding. High-risk, time-critical (pregnancy test + ultrasound + type-and-screen): ESI 2. The borderline tachycardia and low-normal BP are the only objective hints; the acuity lives in the history.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored calibration-trap case
